title: findPagedByNamespace function exceeds 50-line limit (110 lines)
description: findPagedByNamespace is 110 lines long and performs in-memory filtering, sorting, version-constraint matching, and pagination all within a single function body. This makes the function hard to test in isolation and violates the single-responsibility guideline.
recommendation: Extract visibility filtering, tag/full-text filtering, version filtering, and in-memory sorting into private helper functions each under 50 lines.

title: findLatestPublishedReleasesForPlugins queried twice in findPagedByNamespace
description: findLatestPublishedReleasesForPlugins is called once at line 108 to build pluginIdsWithPublishedRelease and again at line 156 inside the version-filter branch. The second call re-fetches the same data from the database unnecessarily.
recommendation: Compute latestReleases once outside the version-filter branch and derive pluginIdsWithPublishedRelease from it rather than issuing a second database query.

title: Non-null assertion on namespace.id inside findPagedByNamespace
description: Asserting namespace.id non-null will throw NullPointerException if the namespace entity has a null ID. The surrounding code already uses mapNotNull on plugin IDs, so the asymmetry is surprising and the failure mode is a generic NPE with no context.
recommendation: Replace the non-null assertion with requireNotNull(namespace.id) and a meaningful error message.

title: Broad catch(Exception) used for control flow in resolveVisibility
description: CatalogController.resolveVisibility uses two consecutive try/catch(Exception) blocks to probe role membership by catching ForbiddenException as a control-flow signal. Catching the broad Exception type may swallow unexpected runtime errors such as database connectivity failures, silently downgrading the caller to PUBLIC visibility instead of surfacing the error.
recommendation: Use a dedicated boolean helper on NamespaceAuthorizationService such as hasRole that returns true/false without throwing, replacing exception-driven control flow.

title: Non-null assertion on SecurityContextHolder authentication in ManagementController
description: SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication is asserted non-null in every ManagementController handler without a prior null check. Other controllers such as NamespaceController explicitly check for null and throw UnauthorizedException, making this inconsistent.
recommendation: Replace the non-null assertion with requireNotNull or a shared helper that returns UnauthorizedException when null, consistent with NamespaceController.

title: Non-null assertion on authentication in ReviewsController without null guard
description: ReviewsController asserts authentication non-null in approveRelease and rejectRelease without a null guard. The inconsistency with NamespaceController which does check for null creates a maintenance trap if auth is ever null in a future filter-chain change.
recommendation: Add a shared AuthHelper.requireAuthentication() that converts null auth to UnauthorizedException consistently across all controllers.

title: Non-null assertion on authentication duplicated across controller layer (7 occurrences)
description: Three controllers (ManagementController, ReviewsController, AccessKeyController) use SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication with non-null assertion without a null guard at least seven times across the codebase.
recommendation: Centralise authentication extraction into a shared extension function such as SecurityContextHolder.requireAuthentication() that throws UnauthorizedException on null.

title: Non-null assertion on passwordEncoder.encode result repeated in UserService and AdminInitializationRunner
description: passwordEncoder.encode(password) is non-null-asserted on lines 59, 72, and 93 of UserService and line 71 of AdminInitializationRunner. BCryptPasswordEncoder never returns null in practice but the assertion silences the compiler type checker. AccessKeyService.create already uses requireNotNull correctly.
recommendation: Use requireNotNull(passwordEncoder.encode(password)) with a meaningful message consistently throughout the codebase.

title: Non-null assertion on release.id inside downloadArtifact
description: releaseRepository.incrementDownloadCount on release.id will throw NPE if the entity is in a detached state with a null ID, with no contextual error message about which release or what operation was in progress.
recommendation: Replace the non-null assertion with requireNotNull(release.id) and a meaningful error message.

title: Broad catch(Exception) in storage methods swallows unexpected non-IO errors
description: All three storage methods catch the root Exception type to wrap it in ArtifactStorageException. Catching Exception also catches ClassCastException, OutOfMemoryError parent hierarchy, and other unrelated failures, making root-cause diagnosis harder.
recommendation: Catch java.io.IOException specifically which covers all file I/O failures and let other unchecked exceptions propagate naturally.

title: Broad catch(Exception) in deleteStorageArtifacts swallows non-IO errors
description: The catch block in deleteStorageArtifacts catches all Exception types. A programming error inside storageService.delete such as a NullPointerException would be logged as a warning and silently ignored rather than propagating as a service error.
recommendation: Narrow the catch to IOException or ArtifactStorageException to ensure only expected storage failures are swallowed during namespace deletion.

title: Broad catch(Exception) in anonymizeIpAddress with fail-open privacy behaviour
description: anonymizeIpAddress catches all Exception types and returns the original non-anonymised IP on failure. A bug in the IP parsing code would silently store raw IP addresses even when anonymisation is configured, violating the operator privacy expectation without any clear error signal.
recommendation: Catch UnknownHostException specifically. If the fail-open behaviour is intentional document it explicitly and consider emitting a warning metric.

title: Plaintext initial admin password logged at INFO level via SLF4J
description: AdminInitializationRunner logs the generated initial admin password at INFO level. INFO is commonly forwarded to centralised log aggregation (Datadog, ELK, CloudWatch) where it may be stored indefinitely. The password is visible to anyone with log-read access.
recommendation: Log at WARN level with an explicit note that it is a one-time secret. Consider printing to stdout/stderr outside the logging framework to avoid log-aggregation forwarding.

title: Two isSuperadmin overloads with inconsistent caller usage and bypassed key-prefix guard
description: The class declares a public isSuperadmin(Authentication) at line 126 and a private isSuperadmin(String) at line 147. requireRole on line 104 calls the private String overload directly bypassing the key prefix guard applied in the public overload. A future naming change could admit an access-key principal as superadmin via the private path.
recommendation: Consolidate both overloads into a single isSuperadmin(Authentication) that applies all guards consistently and eliminate the private String overload being called from requireRole.

title: NamespaceAlreadyExistsException locally caught and converted to empty 409 response body
description: createNamespace catches NamespaceAlreadyExistsException and returns ResponseEntity.status(409).build() with no body. Every other conflict case is handled by GlobalExceptionHandler which returns a structured ErrorResponse. Callers receive an empty 409 with no machine-readable error detail.
recommendation: Remove the local try/catch and let GlobalExceptionHandler.handleConflict handle NamespaceAlreadyExistsException uniformly with a structured body.

title: Security-sensitive JPA entity fields lack mutation guidance in KDoc
description: UserEntity declares every field as var including isSuperadmin and passwordHash. Accidental direct mutation outside UserService bypasses audit logic and token revocation. There is no documentation indicating these fields must only be modified through the service layer.
recommendation: Add KDoc comments on isSuperadmin and passwordHash explicitly noting they must only be modified through UserService to preserve audit trail and revocation semantics.

title: update() silently no-ops and calls save when all nullable parameters are null
description: update() uses safe-call let chains to apply changes. A caller who passes all-null params will silently no-op and still invoke pluginRepository.save with an unchanged entity, wasting a database round-trip and giving no feedback.
recommendation: Document the no-op behaviour or return a boolean or typed result indicating whether any field was actually changed.

title: lateinit var bootSnapshot with reflection-based isInitialized guard in production code
description: bootSnapshot is declared lateinit and guarded by isInitialized in listAll. Using lateinit for a value set exactly once at PostConstruct with a reflection guard is an anti-pattern when a nullable val with a simple null-coalesce achieves the same result without reflection.
recommendation: Replace lateinit var bootSnapshot with a nullable val bootSnapshot and use bootSnapshot or cache.get() in listAll.

title: Repository injected directly into AdminUserController bypassing service layer
description: AdminUserController injects NamespaceMemberRepository directly (line 40) to call countByUserSubject inside the toDto private extension. Controllers should delegate all persistence access to services not repositories, and this creates a hidden persistence dependency invisible to the service layer.
recommendation: Move countByUserSubject into UserService as countMemberships(username) and use the service in DTO conversion.

title: Two repositories injected directly into CatalogController bypassing service layer
description: CatalogController injects PluginReleaseRepository and NamespaceRepository directly and calls five distinct repository methods inside the controller, scattering data access responsibilities across the controller layer.
recommendation: Move the multi-repository orchestration logic into PluginService or a dedicated CatalogQueryService keeping controllers free of direct repository dependencies.

title: maxBytes cast to Int may silently overflow for large upload size configurations
description: maxBytes.toInt() on line 131 silently overflows to a negative number if maxBytes exceeds Int.MAX_VALUE. The current ceiling of 1024 MB keeps this safe but a future change to that ceiling would introduce a silent overflow without any compile-time or runtime warning.
recommendation: Assert maxBytes is within Int range before the cast with a clear error message, or use a Long-safe alternative.

title: parseManifest and parseProperties are near-identical structural duplication (DRY violation)
description: parseManifest (lines 25-60) and parseProperties (lines 62-96) duplicate almost identical field extraction and PlugwerkDescriptor construction logic differing only in whether they read from Manifest attributes or Properties. Any new descriptor field requires changes in both methods.
recommendation: Extract a private buildDescriptor factory and have both parse methods call it after reading their respective source format.

title: Double role-check in updatePlugin creates cognitive complexity
description: updatePlugin calls requireRole(MEMBER) and then conditionally calls requireRole(ADMIN) for status changes. The double security-context lookup and conditional extra check add maintenance overhead and are easy to accidentally break when adding new fields to PluginUpdateRequest.
recommendation: Use a single requireRole call with the minimum required role determined upfront from the request payload, or document the two-step check with a comment explaining correctness.

title: Post-delete reload of all releases to check emptiness instead of count query
description: After deleting a release delete() reloads all remaining releases via findAllByPluginOrderByCreatedAtDesc solely to check remaining.isEmpty(). This fires an additional SELECT returning full entity data when a COUNT query would suffice.
recommendation: Replace the post-delete reload with a repository count query to avoid loading entity data for an emptiness check.

title: ALPHANUMERIC companion object value is a MutableList used for read-only indexed access
description: ALPHANUMERIC on line 50 is a concatenation of character ranges which produces a MutableList. It is used only for indexed reads but exposing a mutable list from a companion object is an immutability smell.
recommendation: Convert ALPHANUMERIC to explicit List<Char> or use a CharArray or String constant.

title: OIDC decoder initialisation failure logged with string interpolation losing stack trace
description: The onFailure block logs the exception message via string interpolation. This discards the full stack trace making it impossible to diagnose connection or configuration failures for OIDC provider initialisation in production.
recommendation: Pass the exception as the last argument to log.warn following the SLF4J convention to include the full stack trace.

title: artifactKey construction does not guard against null namespace.id
description: The artifactKey is built as a string using plugin.namespace.id. If namespace.id is null before persistence this produces the literal string null as part of a valid-looking storage key with no error.
recommendation: Assert plugin.namespace.id is non-null before building the key using requireNotNull.

title: Broad catch(Exception) in download() may swallow thread interruption signals
description: download() catches Exception at line 52 to call deleteSilently before rethrowing. This broad catch also intercepts InterruptedException and other signals potentially masking cancellation in calling thread pools.
recommendation: Catch IOException for I/O cleanup and rethrow other exceptions directly without catching them.

title: install() outer catch(Exception) converts all errors to InstallResult.Failure hiding programming bugs
description: The outer catch(ex: Exception) in install() converts NullPointerException and other unexpected exceptions into InstallResult.Failure with ex.message which may be null. Callers receive an opaque failure with no way to distinguish I/O errors from programming bugs.
recommendation: Distinguish expected network or IO failures from unexpected runtime exceptions and let unexpected exceptions propagate unchecked.

title: Non-null assertion on release.id in UpdateCheckService.toDto
description: PluginReleaseEntity.toDto uses non-null assertion on id with no contextual error message if the entity is in a transient or detached state with a null ID.
recommendation: Replace non-null assertion with requireNotNull(id) and a meaningful error message.

title: Non-null assertion on entity.id in PluginMapper.toDto without explicit guard
description: PluginMapper.toDto asserts entity.id non-null making the non-null assumption invisible to future maintainers calling the mapper in new paths.
recommendation: Replace non-null assertion with requireNotNull(entity.id) and a meaningful error message.

title: Non-null assertion on id duplicated across three toDto extension functions
description: The id non-null assertion pattern in entity.toDto extensions appears in AccessKeyController (line 89), ReviewsController (line 52), and AdminUserController (line 82) without guards.
recommendation: Consolidate the id-assertion pattern across all toDto extensions using requireNotNull for meaningful error messages.

title: Fully qualified java.util.UUID references instead of file-level import used in multiple locations
description: java.util.UUID appears fully qualified in PagedCatalogResult at line 75 and at lines 105, 114, 166, and in CatalogController line 92, while the file also uses imported UUID in other places.
recommendation: Add a top-level import for java.util.UUID and remove redundant fully-qualified references.

title: Unchecked casts on raw Object array from JPQL sumDownloadCountsByPluginIds projection
description: The associate block performs unchecked casts (it[0] as UUID) and (it[1] as Long). A JPQL type mismatch such as Integer instead of Long would throw ClassCastException at runtime. The same pattern occurs in CatalogController line 100.
recommendation: Use a dedicated JPQL projection DTO or a typed repository method to eliminate raw Object array casts.

title: validate() function body is 115 lines and will grow with each new descriptor field
description: The validate function collects violations across 115 lines of sequential if and let blocks. Adding new descriptor fields will continue to grow this function beyond the 50-line guideline.
recommendation: Split into per-category validators such as validateIdentity, validateUrls, validateTags, validateDependencies called from a short main entry point.
